compiled_state.title: Rebase-aware test gate — detect rebase state and adjust hash verification
compiled_state.description:
## Context

During automated rebase operations in merge-to-main.sh (squash-rebase recovery, git pull --rebase), files modified on the target branch get staged even though the worktree never changed them and they are not conflicted. The pre-commit test gate (pre-commit-test-gate.sh) sees these staged files, finds associated tests, and blocks the commit because no test-gate-status was recorded for them. This forces unnecessary test runs for unchanged code, stalling agent workflows. Agent orchestrators running merge-to-main.sh in worktree sessions encounter this on every squash-rebase recovery cycle. The test gate already solves this exact problem for merge commits via MERGE_HEAD detection and incoming-only file filtering but has no equivalent handling for REBASE_HEAD.

## Success Criteria

1. When REBASE_HEAD is present during a commit, the test gate identifies files changed only on the rebase target (not by the worktree branch) and excludes them from gate enforcement
2. Files modified by both the worktree branch and the rebase target (conflicted and resolved) remain subject to gate enforcement
3. The rebase detection reads the onto ref from .git/rebase-merge/onto or .git/rebase-apply/onto to compute the correct merge base
4. compute-diff-hash.sh produces a stable hash during rebase by excluding incoming-only files from the diff, consistent with its existing merge-commit behavior
5. The squash-rebase recovery path in merge-to-main.sh completes without test gate false-positives on incoming-only files
6. Test suite includes bash tests that simulate REBASE_HEAD state with incoming-only files (gate passes), conflict-resolved files (gate enforces), and missing onto file (gate fails open) mirroring the existing MERGE_HEAD test coverage pattern

## Dependencies

None. Soft ordering note: should complete before 5ee9-143e (Redesign merge state detection) to avoid rework if that epic refactors the MERGE_HEAD filtering pattern this epic mirrors.

## Approach

Mirror the existing MERGE_HEAD incoming-only file filtering pattern in pre-commit-test-gate.sh and compute-diff-hash.sh: detect REBASE_HEAD, read the onto ref to determine the rebase target, compute which files are incoming-only, and exclude them from gate enforcement and hash computation.

comments.body:
### Scenario Analysis (9 surviving scenarios)

Round 1 (7 scenarios):
- REBASE_HEAD present but onto file missing (high, runtime): Gate must fail open when onto file is absent
- rebase-apply vs rebase-merge layout mismatch (high, runtime): Must check both directories
- Conflicted files misclassified as incoming-only (critical, runtime): Must use worktree branch pre-rebase tip (not detached HEAD)
- Review gate hash inconsistency post-rebase (high, runtime): compute-diff-hash.sh must exclude incoming-only files symmetrically
- Unresolvable onto ref (medium, runtime): Must fail open on git merge-base failure
- ORIG_HEAD unreliable for branch-tip identification (high, config): Read orig-head from rebase state directory
- Shallow clone merge-base failure (high, config): Must fail open, consistent with existing MERGE_HEAD handling

Round 2 (2 additional scenarios):
- Hash divergence between review-time and commit-time (high, runtime): compute-diff-hash.sh must add REBASE_HEAD handling symmetrically so hashes match across the review/commit boundary
- Per-worktree git dir resolution (medium, runtime): Must use git rev-parse --git-dir to resolve correct per-worktree rebase state paths in linked worktrees

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): re-triggered via gate
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 9 (7 round 1 + 2 round 2)
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: scenario analysis re-run 1 time(s)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Adversarial review: 5 findings, 4 accepted. Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-345ec2e15ec2e3e0/adversarial-review-w22-jucp.json
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Epic complete: all 4 stories closed, 13 implementation tasks completed across 5 batches. REBASE_HEAD handling added to 6 scripts symmetric with existing MERGE_HEAD. 17 behavioral tests cover filtering, enforcement, failsafe, prune guard, hash stability, and classifier detection. CLAUDE.md updated. CI failures are pre-existing on main (unrelated).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
